text_original: The skilful traveller leaves no traces of his wheels or footsteps;
text_normalized: The skilful traveller leaves no traces of his wheels or footsteps;

text_original: the skilful speaker says nothing that can be found fault with or blamed;
text_normalized: the skilful speaker says nothing that can be found fault with or blamed;

text_original: the skilful reckoner uses no tallies;
text_normalized: the skilful reckoner uses no tallies;

text_original: the skilful closer needs no bolts or bars, while to open what he has shut will be impossible;
text_normalized: the skilful closer needs no bolts or bars, while to open what he has shut will be impossible;

text_original: the skilful binder uses no strings or knots, while to unloose what he has bound will be impossible.
text_normalized: the skilful binder uses no strings or knots, while to unloose what he has bound will be impossible.

text_original: In the same way the sage is always skilful at saving men, and so he does not cast away any man;
text_normalized: In the same way the sage is always skilful at saving men, and so he does not cast away any man;

text_original: he is always skilful at saving things, and so he does not cast away anything.
text_normalized: he is always skilful at saving things, and so he does not cast away anything.

text_original: This is called 'Hiding the light of his procedure.'
text_normalized: This is called 'Hiding the light of his procedure.'

text_original: Therefore the man of skill is a master (to be looked up to) by him who has not the skill;
text_normalized: Therefore the man of skill is a master (to be looked up to) by him who has not the skill;

text_original: and he who has not the skill is the helper of (the reputation of) him who has the skill.
text_normalized: and he who has not the skill is the helper of (the reputation of) him who has the skill.

text_original: If the one did not honour his master, and the other did not rejoice in his helper, an (observer), though intelligent, might greatly err about them.
text_normalized: If the one did not honour his master, and the other did not rejoice in his helper, an (observer), though intelligent, might greatly err about them.

text_original: This is called 'The utmost degree of mystery.'
text_normalized: This is called 'The utmost degree of mystery.'
